description_long: This is a classic biplane with a green fuselage and dual stacked wings, reminiscent of early 20th-century aircraft. The airplane features a prominent radial engine at the nose, with a visible propeller and exposed engine details. The wings are straight and parallel, supported by struts and wires, and the landing gear consists of two main wheels and a small tail wheel. The tail section includes a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers, forming a conventional tail design. The cockpit is situated just behind the upper wing, appearing to be open and likely single-seat. The overall construction suggests a lightweight metal or wood frame with fabric or metal skin, typical of vintage biplanes.
description: A green vintage biplane with dual stacked wings, a radial engine and propeller, and an open cockpit behind the upper wing.
description_view_0: Front view showing the radial engine, propeller, and the stacked biplane wings with landing gear visible below.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the elongated fuselage, open cockpit, and the taildragger landing gear configuration.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the tail section with single vertical and horizontal stabilizers, and the dual wings from behind.
description_view_3: Right side view similar to the left, showing the propeller, cockpit, and tail structure.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Biplane configuration with two straight, parallel wings stacked vertically; no winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single front-mounted radial piston engine with a two-blade propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Taildragger configuration with two main fixed wheels and a small tail wheel.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, open cockpit located just behind the upper wing, likely single-seat.
